meta.subtitle: Coordinates, Ratio & Proportion Adventure
meta.description: An interactive, mission-based multimedia learning resource that teaches Year 5 pupils coordinates, ratio and proportion through gamified Malaysian real-world contexts.

milestones.label: Coordinates
milestones.tagline: Map the world with X and Y!

missions.title: Coordinates Mission
missions.tagline: Map the world with X and Y!
missions.standard: Standard 7.1.1 — Determine the horizontal and vertical distance between two coordinates.

performanceLevels.desc: State the horizontal axis (x) and vertical axis (y).
Explain the steps to find the distance between two points.
Justify the answer using reasoning and examples.

learn.intro: A coordinate tells us an exact position on a grid using two numbers (x, y). The x-value moves left↔right along the horizontal axis, and the y-value moves up↕down along the vertical axis.
concepts: The horizontal axis is the x-axis; the vertical axis is the y-axis.
A point is written as (x, y) — always x first, then y.
Horizontal distance = difference between the two x-values.
Vertical distance = difference between the two y-values.
learn.interactivePlaceholder: Native interactive coordinate grid helper will be embedded here.

explore.note: Drag the points and watch the horizontal and vertical distances change in real time.

practice.intro: Check your understanding with these quick questions.

questions.q: In the coordinate (4, 7), which number is on the x-axis?
Find the horizontal distance between (2, 3) and (8, 3).

missions.title: Ratio Mission
missions.standard: Standard 7.2.1 — Represent ratio involving Part to Part, Part to Whole, and Whole to Part.

performanceLevels.desc: State a ratio from a given situation.
Represent Part to Part, Part to Whole and Whole to Part.
Solve and explain ratio problems in real contexts.

learn.intro: A ratio compares two or more quantities. For example, if a 'Sirap Bandung' uses 2 cups of syrup to 5 cups of milk, the ratio of syrup to milk is 2 : 5.
concepts: Part to Part — compares one part with another part (syrup : milk = 2 : 5).
Part to Whole — compares one part with the total (syrup : total = 2 : 7).
Whole to Part — compares the total with one part (total : milk = 7 : 5).
learn.interactivePlaceholder: Interactive ratio bar/mixer will be embedded here.

explore.note: Adjust the bars to see how Part-to-Part and Part-to-Whole ratios relate.

practice.intro: Check your understanding with these quick questions.

questions.q: A box has 3 red and 4 blue marbles. What is the ratio red : blue?
Using the same box, what is red : total (Part to Whole)?

missions.title: Proportion Mission
missions.standard: Standard 7.3.1 — Determine the unknown value using proportion.

performanceLevels.desc: Set up an equivalent proportion from a situation.
Determine and justify the unknown value.

learn.intro: A proportion shows that two ratios are equal. If 2 satay sticks cost RM3, then 4 satay sticks cost RM6 — the ratios 2 : 3 and 4 : 6 are equal.
concepts: Two equal ratios form a proportion: a : b = c : d.
Multiply or divide both parts by the same number to keep it equal.
Use cross-relationships to find an unknown value.
learn.interactivePlaceholder: Interactive proportion solver will be embedded here.

explore.note: Scale the recipe and watch both quantities change in proportion.

practice.intro: Check your understanding with these quick questions.

questions.q: If 2 satay sticks cost RM3, how much do 6 satay sticks cost?
Which pair of ratios forms a proportion?

finalChallenge.title: Pesta Ria Sekolah
finalChallenge.subtitle: The Malaysian School Carnival Simulation
finalChallenge.intro: Welcome to Pesta Ria Sekolah! Use everything you learned to help run the carnival. Each stall is a real-world mission combining coordinates, ratio and proportion.

stages.name: Gerai Satay Setup
stages.desc: Read the carnival map and place the satay stall at the correct coordinate using horizontal and vertical distance.

stages.name: Mix Sirap Bandung
stages.desc: Use the ratio sliders to mix syrup and milk in the correct 2 : 5 ratio for a perfect batch of Sirap Bandung.

stages.name: Pesta Ria Feast
stages.desc: A crowd of teachers arrived! Use the unitary method to scale the recipe up for 20 guests.

stage1.title: Gerai Satay Setup
stage1.mission: The Gerai Satay must be placed 3 units horizontally to the right and 2 units vertically up from the Pentas Utama (1, 2). Click on the correct coordinate!
stage1.successTemplate: Tepat sekali! Coordinate ({x},{y}) is correct. Horizontal distance = {dx} units, Vertical distance = {dy} units!

stage3.title: Pesta Ria Feast
stage3.scenario: A massive crowd of teachers just arrived at the booth! The standard recipe serves 4 people. Scale it up for 20 unexpected guests using proportion.
stage3.hint: Tip: 20 guests is 5 times more than 4 guests! Multiply both ingredients by 5.
stage3.successBody: You scaled the recipe perfectly using the unitary method. The whole staffroom is sipping your Sirap Bandung!

strengths: Data-driven content architecture lets teachers edit lessons and quizzes without touching code.
Multimedia integration (video, GeoGebra, H5P) supports varied learner preferences.
Gamified roadmap with unlock progression sustains pupil motivation and autonomy.
improvements: Add automated formative feedback and progress analytics for the teacher.
Provide Bahasa Melayu / English bilingual toggle for wider accessibility.
Introduce adaptive difficulty based on pupil performance.
justifications: Aligned to KPPB (Kemahiran Pemikiran Aras Tinggi) by scaffolding PL1→PL3 within each mission.
Constructivist 'Learn → Explore → Practice' flow encourages active knowledge building.
Real-world Malaysian carnival context strengthens contextual and cross-cultural relevance.

dskp.desc: Determine horizontal and vertical distance between two coordinates.
Represent ratio: Part to Part, Part to Whole, Whole to Part.
Solve daily-life problems involving coordinates, ratio and proportion.
